---
title: "can't change screen resolution"
date: 2008-07-15
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by erans on 2008-07-15
I installed xorg-driver-fglrx and it reduced my screen res to 640x800 and I wasn't able to change it. I removed it and my screen is still locked at that resolution and I can't change it back to what it was from the preferences (1280x1024). Any ideas?

---

### Post by TSS on 2008-07-15
Can you post your xorg.conf here?

---

### Post by erans on 2008-07-15
Sure, here it is. 

```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by failsafeDexconf, using
# values from the debconf database and some overrides to use vesa mode.
#
# You should use dexconf or another such tool for creating a "real" xorg.conf
# For example:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
	Boardname	"vesa"
	Busid		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Driver		"vesa"
	Screen	0
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
	Vendorname	"Plug 'n' Play"
	Modelname	"Plug 'n' Play"
  modeline  "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -vsync -hsync
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Virtual	640	480
		Modes		"640x480@60"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  screen 0 "Default Screen" 0 0
EndSection
Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
	Load		"GLcore"
	Load		"v4l"
EndSection
Section "ServerFlags"
EndSection
```

---

### Post by babyrajT on 2008-07-15
Hi ,

Switch to shell prompt and type sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
This will open the setting for changing the resolution.If it doesn't work type sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg this will reset the xorg file, and restart the system.hope this will help you.

---

### Post by TSS on 2008-07-15
Reconfiguring your xserver should work.  If you look at your xorg.conf file, there problem is here:
```

	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Virtual	640	480
		Modes		"640x480@60"
	EndSubSection

```
The only mode contained in your xorg.conf file is 640x480, which is why you are stuck at it.

---

### Post by erans on 2008-07-15
It worked! Thanks guys. :)

---
